commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=42c6804989eeebbc4896266b68480c45bf4ea626)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes OpenRouter DeepSeek V4 request shaping to keep nested `reasoning.effort`, remove DeepSeek-native `thinking` and top-level `reasoning_effort`, adjust the core non-native compat guard, and add regression tests and provider docs.

PR surface: Source +33, Tests +58, Docs 0. Total +91 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main can build nested OpenRouter `reasoning.effort` and then apply a DeepSeek V4 wrapper that adds top-level `reasoning_effort` on the same payload. I did not run a live OpenRouter repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review should require the remaining payload-shape proof and docs update; automation cannot supply the contributor's live OpenRouter evidence.

target.title: fix: avoid DeepSeek-native thinking on OpenRouter V4
target.body: Fixes #97196 ## What Problem This Solves Fixes an issue where users running OpenRouter-routed DeepSeek V4 models could hit a 400 response because OpenClaw sent both OpenRouter reasoning controls and DeepSeek-native thinking controls in the same request. ## Why This Change Was Made OpenRouter DeepSeek V4 requests now stay on the OpenRouter reasoning payload shape (`reasoning.effort`) instead of adding DeepSeek-native `thinking` / `reasoning_effort` fields after the provider wrapper runs. The DeepSeek V4 replay repair still backfills `reasoning_content` only when OpenRouter reasoning remains enabled, and the core fallback now respects detected non-native thinking formats so unowned DeepSeek-compatible proxy routes keep their native fallback without breaking OpenRouter. ## User Impact Users can use `openrouter/deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash` and `openrouter/deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro` without the request being rejected for conflicting reasoning fields. `/think max` and stale max overrides still map to OpenRouter's supported `xhigh` effort. ## Evidence - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/openrouter/index.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/extra-params.deepseek-v4-thinking-format.test.ts` - `extensions/openrouter/index.test.ts`: 43 passed - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/extra-params.deepseek-v4-thinking-format.test.ts`: 9 passed - `git diff --check` - `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` - clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported Real behavior proof still to add before ready-for-review: - Screenshot of a live OpenRouter DeepSeek V4 request succeeding from OpenClaw. - Screenshot or redacted log line showing the outbound request/request debug no longer contains top-level `reasoning_effort` or `thinking`, while `reasoning.effort` is present. Live OpenRouter DeepSeek V4 proof: the request reached openrouter for deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash, returned status=200, and produced the expected reply without the previous conflicting reasoning-fields error.
target.author: nianjiuzst

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: NianJiuZst
